roles.core: central bot, D1 authority, payment callbacks, report outbox, safe update controller and Durable Object live usage engine
roles.edge: reseller bots, customer miniapps and imported-subscription lifecycle with fail-open Telegram webhook acknowledgement
roles.processor: optional advanced schedules, backups, campaigns and background reports

features: Telegram central webhook always acknowledges with HTTP 200 after containing an internal failure, preventing retry storms and total bot lockup
reseller Telegram webhooks also fail open with HTTP 200 when Core or remote D1 is temporarily unavailable
normal user requests never trigger GitHub or Cloudflare deployment checks
Cron update errors are isolated from the rest of scheduled maintenance jobs
release_rollouts, system_error_center and repair_runs are optional observability tables and cannot block deployment or bot runtime
staged deployment continues to preserve Core when Edge or Processor does not deploy successfully
error-center and repair-all screens operate in degraded mode instead of crashing when tracking tables are unavailable
database schema revision remains 3.3.3 because this rescue release adds no schema change, avoiding a heavy migration on the recovery request
retains all 3.3 growth, loyalty, service-health, D1 self-healing and previous 3.2 fixes

deployment.emergency_recovery: paste or upload core/worker.js to the existing Core Worker and deploy once; then upload this ZIP through the restored central bot
deployment.automatic_provisioning: supported from the central bot ZIP uploader after Core recovery
deployment.cron: existing central cron may remain; update failures are isolated and live usage stays on Durable Object when configured

limitations: a Worker whose D1 or Service Binding was manually removed still requires that binding to be restored in Cloudflare settings
the emergency HTTP 200 response prevents Telegram retry storms but cannot complete a business operation while its required dependency is unavailable
representative low-balance automation does not charge a bank card without a payment mandate
automatic failover must be explicitly enabled by the reseller
